steps.title: Verify Your STEM Eligibility
steps.description: Confirm that your degree is on the official STEM Designated Degree Program list and you have valid OPT.
tips: Check the STEM Designated Degree Program list on ICE website
Your degree must match exactly with the CIP code
You must currently be on approved OPT
Talk to your DSO to confirm eligibility

steps.title: Find an E-Verify Employer
steps.description: Your employer must be enrolled in E-Verify to sponsor your STEM OPT extension.
tips: Employer must be enrolled in E-Verify
Job must be directly related to your STEM degree
Part-time work (20+ hours/week) is acceptable
You can have multiple employers if all use E-Verify

steps.title: Complete Form I-983 with Your Employer
steps.description: Work with your employer to complete the Training Plan (Form I-983). This outlines your learning objectives.
tips: Both you and employer must sign
Learning objectives must relate to your degree
Include supervisor contact information
Employer must commit to evaluation requirements

steps.title: Request DSO Recommendation in SEVIS
steps.description: Bring your completed I-983 to your DSO. They will recommend the STEM extension in SEVIS and issue a new I-20.
tips: Schedule appointment early
Bring completed I-983 and current EAD
DSO will update your SEVIS record
Get your new I-20 with STEM recommendation

steps.timing: Within 60 days of receiving new I-20
steps.title: Prepare STEM OPT Application (Form I-765)
steps.description: Complete Form I-765 with the STEM OPT extension category code.
tips: Category is (c)(3)(C) for STEM extension
Include new I-20 with STEM recommendation
Copy of I-983 Training Plan
Filing fee ($410 as of 2026)

steps.title: File Application with USCIS
steps.description: Mail your complete STEM OPT application to USCIS before your current OPT period expires.
tips: Must be received before OPT expiration
Use certified mail with tracking
Keep copies of everything
You get automatic 180-day extension while pending

steps.title: Wait for New EAD Card
steps.description: USCIS will process your application and mail your new EAD card with the 24-month extension.
tips: Continue working on auto-extension
Track your case online
Update address if you move
Don't leave the US while pending (risky)

steps.title: Submit 6-Month I-983 Evaluation
steps.description: Complete and submit your first I-983 evaluation to your DSO within 10 days of the 6-month mark.
tips: Both you and employer must sign evaluation
Review and update learning objectives
Report any changes in employment
Keep copies for your records

steps.title: Submit 12-Month I-983 Evaluation
steps.description: Complete your second I-983 evaluation and submit to your DSO.
tips: Same process as 6-month evaluation
Update progress on learning objectives
Report any employer or position changes

steps.title: Plan Your Next Steps
steps.description: Start planning for H-1B or other long-term visa options during your STEM extension period.
tips: H-1B registration typically in March
Talk to employer about sponsorship early
Consider consulting an immigration attorney
Keep all documents organized
